frontmatter.description: A repeatable pre-review checklist used before opening any PR over 200 lines: regenerate the lockfile, run the integration suite locally, and re-verify the README quickstart.

asset.body:
Pre-review checklist (PRs over ~200 lines):

1. Regenerate the lockfile when any dependency was touched (`bun install --frozen-lockfile` to verify).
2. Run the full integration suite locally; flakes here block the PR rather than the CI.
3. Re-verify the README quickstart end-to-end on a fresh clone — README rot is the most common review-cycle blocker.
4. Squash mechanical commits before requesting review; reviewers should not have to bisect mid-PR refactors.

Applying this checklist before review consistently shaves one review round on average across the team.

feedback.reason: Caught a missing lockfile entry before the reviewer did
feedback.note: Saved a full round-trip on PR #412.

feedback.reason: Step 3 surfaced README rot in PR #418
feedback.note: Reviewer commented that the proactive fix made the PR approvable on first pass.

feedback.reason: Used as the rubric for the new-hire PR-workflow doc

humanGrade.rationale: Stable, repeatable process knowledge with three independent positive signals. The judge should produce a clean lesson; queued is the correct outcome.
